PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Aaron Roy Feldman appearing via zoom for and with Deidra Lintz appeared via zoom.
Matthew Talbot appearing via zoom for and with Interested party Charlotte Riles appeared via zoom.

The Court noted that no one is present for Lorna Hayes or Gisele Lintz. Mr. Talbot's request for a continuance is denied. All oral objections are overruled. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the Court's intent to grant. Deidra Lintz is to be appointed as the Administrator of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $300,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for April 16, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Frank Mahoney III is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-11-2023 Continued To Mar-22-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Aaron Feldman, Esq. via Zoom and Marla Myint, Esq. via Zoom, for Diedra Lintz (Petitioner) also via Zoom. Lorna Lintz Hayes (Objector/Interested Party) in propria persona, via Zoom. Linda Todd (Cousin/Interested Party) in propria persona, in person.

The Court addressed the petition and stated that it had given Ms. Hayes (Lintz Hayes) three months to file objections. Ms. Lintz Hayes stated that she was unable to obtain counsel. Ms. Todd stated that she was unable to obtain counsel due to the recent loss of family members, that attorney's were not calling her back, and that the attorneys were probably out of the office for the holidays. The Court stated that the petition has been pending for six months, that six months is a long time, and noted that the Mr. Lintz has been deceased for 27 years. The Court addressed Ms. Lintz Hayes and Ms. Todd stating that if each of them can't find an attorney that they will need to prepare and file their own objections. The Court stated that the petition requests that Diedra Lintz be appointed to represent the estate of Cleon Lintz with a $300,000 bond and that it needs to know if Ms. Lintz Hayes and Ms. Todd are opposed to those requests and why. The Court stated that it would allow one final continuance.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 22, 2022, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. All objections are to be filed with the Court by March 13, 2023. Anyone objecting to the petition is to appear on March 22, 2023.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Continued To Jan-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Aaron R. Feldman, via Zoom, for Deidra Lintz, Petitioner; Lorna Lintz-Hayes, via Zoom, and Gisele Lintz, in court, in propria persona, interested parties.

The court confirms with Lorna Lintz-Hayes, via Zoom, and Gisele Lintz that they plan on objecting to the petition before the court. The court advises them that they will need to file formal written objections. The potential objectors request to continue this matter to January to allow for the filing of their objections. Mr. Feldman presents to objection to the request for continuance. The court advises that it will be continuing this matter to allow for the filing of objections and advises that the parties must provide notice of the same to all parties who are entitled to notice and courtesy copies of the same must be delivered to the court. The court pointed out the difficult nature of probate proceedings and suggests that the potential objectors consider hiring an attorney accordingly. The court advises the parties that it is inclined to order a full bond in the amount of $2 million regardless of who is appointed.

The matter is continued to Jan-11-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Lozada. Not Reported.
